reviews.review_title: You need to own a accu bow
reviews.review_description:
You need to own a accu bow which is very expensive and then you have to pay for a subscription, which is $80 for lifetime. It’s stupid and it’s overpriced. The owner of the game is rich. Why would he need this much money? 
￼
I cant shoot and for some reason every time i go to look down it shoots for no reason
You can't even shoot. What more do I have to say
i joined the game to my surprise i saw the graphics we're terrible

I really wanted to like this game. I bought the overpriced bow only to then discover you also have to have a subscription or pay 80$ for lifetime access. There is really no way to keep the bow from jolting when released. This caused the arrow to fly in a crazy direction. I tried adjusting the resistance with no change. The graphics are terrible. If you're using a release, the challenges like zombies are near impossible. There is no tutorial to tell you how you are actually supposed to use the bow and sights. You also have to recalibrate everyone. You open the app, which is cumbersome. You can seemingly shoot an animal several times aiming in the exact same spot, but sometimes it will die and, at other times, not. I definitely wouldn't recommend spending about 300$ on this. They should really use the other controller somehow to improve the feedback and accuracy.

As a hunter and father of a young archery enthusiast i was so excited for this bow and game. Well, the game is extreemly hard to get the bow calibrated and then it doesnt hold zero at all. The game it self has nice graphics but good luck ever getting to shoot, time runs out or no matter how hard you try your shot just misses. Its frustrating and a huge disappointment. Also i bought the expensive AF bow to then have to buy a game that is over priced... *0 out of 5 stars WOULD NOT RECOMMEND*

reviews.review_title: I really wanted to like this....
reviews.review_description:
I really don't have anything to say apart from it's a complete waste of money. 

I really wanted this to be good, but it was just awful. 

My package got delivered at 1pm this afternoon, and in 30 minutes, it is back in the box, and I am requesting a refund. 

Great idea and concept, but it falls short on all its promises.

I purchased this toy bow and game for $200.  Imagine my excitement when it was delivered yesterday.  I thought it was a little pricey but I loved the idea and couldn’t wait  to try it out. I was completely shocked when they wanted another $80 for access to the actual game! There was no mention of this in the online advertising or on the packaging. What a scam! Definitely not worth $300. I will be returning mine tomorrow.

reviews.review_title: Really want to like this game
reviews.review_description:
Update: The new version is better. I do more more testing this week. Needs a sensitivity control as I can’t hold it steady enough to keep the string and peep steady enough to be any good at sighting. 

I show bow and arrow as a kid and thought this would be a great way to practice it again. Have thought about buying several time and quickly pulled the trigger when they came out with a VR version. 

The interface is clunky and gameplay is frustrating. Haven’t even attempted a hunt as target practice leaves me frustrated. Arrows randomly shoot and holding it steady is almost impossible. Peep seems to move even after calibration. Calibration has to be done every time No way to save a calibration setting. 

Really hope updates are coming soon as to not regret my purchase

I never write reviews but this warranted a review. I concur with the other reviews that upon the cost of the bow you also must pay for an app. After 30 minutes of play, the lack of realistic backgrounds, the difficulty and inaccuracy of shooting, and the complication of figuring out the game was to be played immediately prompted a no. I’ve tried to cancel my subscription and it doesn’t even light up as an option. I’ll be curious if I can even find a way to return this. Very frustrated. Interesting concept but poorly executed. Hope I can get out of this. Never trust a site where comments are blocked on FB.

It is a great concept i had the original accubow where you use a phone and that is fantastic. It is correctly sensitive to allow for accurate shooting and timing. This however is not that like other reviews the constant calibaration and them hoping it stays calibrated is awful. The sensitivity is either maxed out where breathing makes you shoot or turned way down to the point you cant even get it to shoot. Their is so much delay whem it acutally fires that your arrow is never where you fired at. I wish this was so much better than it is real downer.

Accubow VR is a great concept, and the delivery is hot garbage. In addition to the 200.00 bow that is not capable of offering let-off like a compound would (making it a pretty unrealistic experience even for training purposes) the VR experience has a subscription (which they price in a way that the lifetime sounds like it makes sense on paper but is immediately filled with regret). The team that built the “game” likely has no practical VR experience as they even start off with some hyper-drive space thing that follows your vision around in an attempt to make users motion sick before the logo even shows up, the in-game bow is wildly disproportionate to what you’re holding, the string clumsily smacks the volume buttons on the Quest 3 headset. Overall I love the idea but it just isn’t a good fit. Buyers beware.

Also the review by Matt Pell should be ignored - he is the CEO of AccuBow.

Wish I had something nice to say about this. Was very disappointed in the game. So when I was trying to return it. I was again disappointed by the customer service and return policy. If you return, you have to pay for the shipping. Also you won’t get a full refund. The additional subscription you would have to buy to play the game also won’t be refunded. AccuBow knows they have a crap product and is trying to nickel and dime you to stop the hemorrhaging of losses. You would probably see negative comments on it. But they erase them and block you from their social media. Neat idea, but failed to deliver and all accounts.

I thought this would be really fun and exciting to play.  I ordered it and waited months for it to arive.  We put it all together and calibrated the remotes, that is a complete joke. Very had to do because when you line the beam up to move the peep sight it moves so fast you can't stop in the correct spot.  Then went to try and play.  The sighting system is not even close to accurate. Then when you run out of arrows you have to find the right hand controller so you can restart the game. How stupid. And you only get 6 arrows.   Give unlimited amput of arrows if you are going to make a person take the head set off every 6 arrows to reset the stupid game.  Long story short this is a waste of money. They convince you to buy it and then you have to buy a subscription for 40 a year or lifetime for 79 dollars.  I bet every person bought the lifetime deal and yeah that is dumb.  I really want all my money back and you can have this junk back. So they offered to take the bow back but they deduct 10 percent for restocking fee and they also won't pay the shipping or the game subscription cost.  Accubow is a crappy company.  Do not buy the game it does not work

This had such a great potential but between the $80 subscription and how quirky it is, basically unusable in current state. The setup process is not intuitive and may have been designed by a blind person. The controller disconnected several times potentially because the holder pushes buttons. It feels like this didn’t go through any user feedback before they pushed it out. I have already returned mine and I don’t know how this product survives. Not a single positive review and no comms from the company in making improvement or addressing feedback.

I’ve never used VR but got the meta quest headset just for this. I love how you can customize your bow interface to calibrate the anchor point and peep sight exactly where you want it. The full 360 view and graphics were better than I was expecting. I’m sure the game modes will continue to evolve from AccuBow but I was highly impressed with this first version. I highly recommend to any fellow bowhunters! 👍🏼👍🏼👍🏼👍🏼👍🏼

I usually don't don't do reviews but this was so disappointing. You have to buy the bow which is expensive then you need a subscription which isn't worth it. Game play sucks the animals stay still just walk back and forth, the bow on the screen loses the rear sight and continue have to recalibrate. Hopefully they see these reviews and fix it

As their first release of the software you can tell it is still on the new side. Shooting the 3 target Target archery section needs to have the angles worked on. If you aim for the 20 yards target it always shoots to the right (me being right handed), but if I shoot at 40 yards it is spot on. I tried the 3D archery targets and at all distances they seem to be zeroed. Calibrating the peep sight to your eye is semi complicated in that if you want to do it right, you need to pull on the bow (by hand or if you are using a release), because otherwise when you pull back the peep sight won't line up correctly. Side note the intro scene for the app gives me vertigo, but that is probably more on my end. 

Like others have said 80 bucks for lifetime access is a little high, but if there are posts or anything letting us know what is coming, being developed, etc. then it wouldn't hurt as bad since we know more is coming versus just saying it's 80 bucks and that's it.

I could not get my VR accubow to calibrate. Please make it easier to calibrate. i will try it again later to see if I can get it to calibrate.
Updated my review: from ** to ****
I finally figured out how to use this app, Calibration took about 5-10 seconds; you just need to hold the bow with your left hand and look straight. And this time it works for me. The first time, my bow shoot a little higher than my aim, so I recalibrated it again by holding it a little lower than my shoulder. The graphics are not bad at all compared to other VR games. I played a couple of games like the Zombie and Serious Hunting games. BTW, you press B to get your right control pointer so you can access menu, etc.. Compared to their 2.0 - Carbon Fiber Archery Trainer with phone mount (yes, I owned that too), I think the phone mount is still better, but eventually, hopefully VR version would improve, esp the arrow flying seems awkward sometimes. Earlier, yes, the bow sometimes shoots itself, but this time, I do not have that issue. I bought their lifetime subscription, so not bad at all once I know how to use it.

The idea of this is great, and hopefully, with time, it will improve.  The lack of instructions  was frustrating ( the tutorial is pretty basic).  A dedicated controller module that links to your headset would be much better. Sometimes, the shot won't fire over and over, but then fire for no reason.  Hopefully, with time, there will be some videos explaining everything better.  Overall, I'm not disappointed in the purchase, but I hope development will continue.  Please create some in-depth tutorials.

No Actual Shooting Feel – Since there’s no arrow release, it doesn’t replicate the full experience of shooting a bow.
Application Issues – There are glitches and connectivity issues with the app.
Expensive – Costs around $150–$200, which may seem high for a training device.
Grip & Balance – I find the grip uncomfortable or awkward at first.
It  took a bit for me to get use to (but I am old man approaching 70). Target practice was fun but when you go on hunt it is AMAZING. The 360 view in the woods is unbelievable.

I got the accubow and looked at the trailer for it thinkin it would be good. I didn't know that you would either have to pay $3.99 a week $39.99 a year or $80.00 for a one time purchase. When I lined up th left controller like it said the bow was off target and I didn't know how to line it up correctly. I recommend to not buy this product. We payed $180.00 fi the total bow and the fees. Very overpriced and I expect better. 





                                                                                                   Disclaimer: 
The Accubow 2.0 with a phone is probbably just worse the than the VR game and more expensive.
We have been Accubow fans for a while, and were so happy that this VR bow was now an option. My husband loves it! It is great for older aged kids as well.
This app is awesome! Dangerous game mode is my favorite. So much to do and so realistic. I love it!
